evals.prompt: I want to design the UX for our team invitation feature. The living doc is at plans/team-invitations.md with ## Requirements covering inviting members, role assignment, and revoking invites.
evals.expected_output: Reads the living doc's ## Requirements first and acknowledges its contents. Explores the codebase for existing UI components, design system, and navigation patterns. Conducts a structured UX interview covering all 8 domains (entry points, user flows, screen inventory, state coverage, component mapping, interaction details, accessibility, content/copy) one question at a time with recommendations. Writes ## UX Design into the living doc with step-by-step user flows for each user story (invite, assign role, revoke), screen inventory with all states (empty members list, loading, error, populated), component inventory mapped to existing codebase components, accessibility requirements (focus trapping in invite modal, keyboard nav for member list), and content/copy (button labels, error messages, empty state messaging). Should NOT include data models, API endpoints, database schemas, or system architecture. Should end by suggesting '/architect'.

evals.prompt: Let's design the UX for a dashboard sharing feature. Users need to share dashboards externally via links with granular visibility controls. No PRD exists yet.
evals.expected_output: Checks for a PRD (finds none) and suggests running /define first but proceeds if user wants to continue. Explores codebase for existing sharing patterns, link generation UI, permission controls, and design system components. Conducts a UX-focused interview covering: entry point for sharing (share button location, modal vs panel), the share link creation flow (step-by-step with visibility options), the external viewer experience (what they see, auth-free access), the link management flow (list active links, revoke access), screen inventory with states (no shares yet, active shares, expired links), component mapping, accessibility (focus management in share modal, screen reader for permission toggles), and copy (share button label, permission level descriptions, confirmation messages). Should flag PRD gaps as they arise and track them. Should NOT specify auth token storage, API design, or database models. Should end by suggesting '/architect'.

evals.prompt: design: We're adding AI-powered search to replace keyword search. The living doc is at plans/ai-search.md with ## Requirements. The app uses React with shadcn/ui components.
evals.expected_output: Reads the living doc's ## Requirements and explores the codebase for existing search UI, shadcn/ui component library, and current search patterns. Conducts a UX interview covering: search entry point (existing search bar modification vs new), the search interaction flow (typing, suggestions, submitting, results display), results screen with all states (empty query, loading/streaming results, no results found, error, results with different content types), component mapping to shadcn/ui (Command/Combobox for search input, Card for results, Skeleton for loading), interaction details (keyboard shortcuts for search, arrow key navigation through results, real-time/debounced suggestions), accessibility (search landmark, live region for result count announcements, focus management between input and results), and copy (placeholder text, no-results messaging, AI attribution/confidence indicators). Should reuse existing shadcn/ui components by name. Should NOT specify embedding models, vector databases, retrieval algorithms, or API design. Should end by suggesting '/architect'.

evals.prompt: design: The requirements in plans/notifications.md are missing user stories for bulk actions, the digest email flow, the mute flow, and the notification preferences screen. Let's design the UX anyway.
evals.expected_output: Detects 4 missing user stories from ## Requirements. Under the type-based gap rules, should NOT roll back to /define. Instead, notes each as an assumption, continues the UX interview, and flags all 4 in the PRD Addendum section as 'BLOCKING — backport to Requirements before architect.' Should complete the full UX spec despite the gaps. Should produce a UX spec that covers the missing stories based on reasonable assumptions.

evals.prompt: design: The living doc at plans/permissions.md says 'admins can manage all roles' in ## Scope but says 'only super-admins can assign admin roles' in ## Requirements. These contradict each other. Let's design the UX.
evals.expected_output: Detects a contradictory requirement (admin vs super-admin role management). Should append to ## Rollback Notes in plans/permissions.md with the contradiction as trigger, functional requirements as affected domain, and any existing UX decisions to preserve. Should roll back to /define rather than resolving the contradiction inline. Should NOT continue the UX interview past this point.
